title: Stress Type
description: Identify your stress type and the best way to deal with it.

questions.question: When a deadline approaches, you...

options.text: Stay awake all night thinking of all the things that could go wrong
Procrastinate until the last moment while looking for distractions
Take immediate action, take control, and work quickly

questions.question: Your physical reaction to stress...

options.text: Difficulty sleeping, headaches, thoughts racing
Excessive sleepiness, lethargy, and wanting to just lie down
Heart pounding, muscles tense, wanting to act immediately

questions.question: When conflict arises with someone, you...

options.text: Analyze all sides and arrange words in your head until you're exhausted
Avoid, give silent treatment, hoping the problem will disappear
Confront directly, argue hard so it ends quickly

questions.question: When many tasks pile up at once, you...

options.text: Confused, panicked, and paralyzed not knowing where to start
Do the very easy ones first, or just play on your phone
Make a strict list, robot mode on, and tackle them one by one

questions.question: What is your biggest stress trigger?

options.text: Uncertainty and not knowing what will happen tomorrow
Pressure, heavy expectations, and confrontation from others
Obstacles, inefficiency, and slow-working people

questions.question: When stress hits, you tend to...

options.text: Overthink and get trapped in your own thoughts
Isolate yourself and distract yourself with unimportant things
Become more aggressive, irritable (cranky), and hyper-focused

questions.question: Your most natural way to calm down...

options.text: Vent at length, journal, or seek certainty
Watch movies, play games, sleep, or total 'me time'
Vigorous exercise, cleaning the house, or physical activity

questions.question: When someone criticizes your work...

options.text: I take it to heart and think about it for days
I ignore it, pretend not to care to protect my ego
I get defensive immediately, respond, and prove them wrong

questions.question: Your relationship with work/tasks...

options.text: I always worry that the results won't be perfect enough (anxious perfectionist)
I work just enough, don't want to take on stress
I always give 100% and take over others' tasks if they're slow

questions.question: When facing a sudden change that cancels your plans...

options.text: Extremely stressed thinking of all the messed up new scenarios
Resigned, silently disappointed, and let the flow carry me
Immediately look for alternatives, make plans B, C, and D on the spot

questions.question: In others' eyes, when under pressure you seem...

options.text: Restless, asking many questions, and seeming hesitant
Quiet, cold, hard to reach, and indifferent
Tense, intimidating, rigid, and bossy

questions.question: When everything feels out of control...

options.text: I feel shattered, cry, and imagine the worst
I withdraw emotionally and go into 'numb' mode
I aggressively force to take back whatever little control I can

questions.question: What do you need most when you're very stressed?

options.text: Someone to listen, validate, and give certainty
Space alone without anyone bothering you
Real solutions, resources, and the freedom to act

questions.question: After a long stressful period ends, you...

options.text: Still have residual anxiety and fear the problem will return
Sleep all day and feel lazy to do anything (passive burnout)
Immediately look for new targets/activities because you can't sit still

questions.question: What advice do you most often hear from friends?

options.text: 'Don't overthink it, relax a little.'
'Don't run from the problem, solve it now.'
'Be patient, don't get emotional, take it slow.'

questions.question: Seeing hundreds of unread emails/messages...

options.text: Anxious about missing something important, read them repeatedly
Mark all as read or ignore them completely
Rapidly reply to all with short, fast, firm answers

questions.question: During a presentation or meeting, a technical issue suddenly arises (lights out/projector breaks)...

options.text: Tremble, panic thinking your presentation will be a mess
Feel secretly relieved, hope the presentation is postponed
Immediately improvise speaking without aids, the show must go on

questions.question: Stuck in a total traffic jam when you have an urgent schedule...

options.text: Keep checking the time, stressed thinking of excuses for being late
Accept it, play music, and convince yourself 'not my fault'
Honk the horn, get emotional, look for extreme alternative routes

questions.question: Your partner sends a message: 'We need to have a serious talk tonight.'

options.text: Analyze all your past mistakes from 3 years ago
Reply 'I'm busy working late' to postpone the conversation
Reply 'Let's talk now, what's the problem?'

questions.question: Accidentally making a wrong money transfer...

options.text: Curse your own stupidity endlessly all day
Comfort yourself 'Oh well, consider it a loss for good luck'
Immediately call the bank, go to the branch, handle it right away

questions.question: A holiday arrives, but your project work isn't finished...

options.text: Body on vacation, but mind tortured constantly thinking about work
Turn off phone notifications, completely forget the work world
Bring your laptop to the vacation spot, force yourself to chip away at work

questions.question: A colleague on your team turns out to be very toxic and lazy...

options.text: Keep thinking about it and stress over how to deal with them
Avoid them as far as possible and minimize interaction
Confront them or report them directly to the boss

questions.question: Forgetting to bring your wallet/card when you're already at the checkout...

options.text: Extremely embarrassed, anxious about being stared at, and apologize repeatedly
Cancel the purchase, leave the items, and go home immediately
Quickly find a solution: transfer via HP, QRIS, or call a nearby friend

questions.question: Failing to meet your diet or exercise goal for the week...

options.text: Feel very guilty and consider yourself a total failure
Rationalize the failure and continue eating well
The next day, go for revenge extreme exercise for 3 hours straight

questions.question: WiFi dies suddenly during an online presentation (Zoom)...

options.text: Panic thinking the participants will consider you unprofessional
Wait passively until it comes back, if not, close the laptop
Immediately tether from your phone, restart the modem, get angry at the provider

questions.question: Someone interrupts while you're explaining an important idea...

options.text: Go silent, overthink whether your idea was indeed bad/boring
Stop speaking completely and lose the desire to continue
Interrupt them back: 'Sorry, I wasn't finished speaking.'

questions.question: Your favorite shirt gets coffee spilled on it just before an event...

options.text: Stress, extremely embarrassed imagining everyone will see it
Cover the stain with a bag/jacket and hope no one notices
Immediately run to the bathroom, scrub with soap until clean/buy a new shirt

questions.question: Given responsibility for a new project you've never done before...

options.text: Anxious thinking about the possibility of failing and disappointing the boss
Lazy imagining the added workload
See it as a challenge to be conquered and mastered

questions.question: Seeing a monthly bill unexpectedly higher than estimated...

options.text: Immediately overthink future bankruptcy
Close your eyes, pay the minimum installment, forget it for a while
Open Excel, check transactions, and radically cut next month's budget

questions.question: An unknown number calls repeatedly...

options.text: Afraid it's bad news, a scam, or a bill, just stare at the screen
Reject the call or silence your phone
Answer immediately and greet them with a firm tone demanding identity

questions.question: Phone battery at 2% while you're out and about...

options.text: Worried that there might be an emergency or someone important looking for you
Accept it, let it die, consider it a 'digital detox' for a while
Run looking for an outlet, borrow a friend's power bank, or stop by a nearby cafe

questions.question: You're caught in a small lie by a friend...

options.text: Panic, look for excuses and long explanations to justify yourself
Smile awkwardly and immediately change the subject
Admit it openly or even turn defensive

questions.question: Your food order at a restaurant is wrong and not what you asked for...

options.text: Stay silent, afraid of bothering the waiter or causing a scene
Eat it as is, too lazy to wait any longer
Call the waiter firmly and ask for a replacement as ordered

questions.question: You realize your friend group is hanging out without inviting you...

options.text: Analyze yourself: 'Did I do something wrong to them?'
Don't care, better find your own activity or play games
Immediately message the group: 'Hey, hanging out without inviting me?'

questions.question: Your car/motorbike keys are left inside or lost...

options.text: Curse your own stupidity repeatedly while imagining the hassle
Sit limply, call home to have someone handle it
Find a wire, force open the window, or call a locksmith right away

questions.question: Missing your flight or train schedule...

options.text: Cry/panic lamenting the wasted money and ruined plans
Go home and sleep, consider it not your fortune
Immediately go to the counter/open your phone to buy the next available ticket at any price

questions.question: Your investment or savings suffer a big loss...

options.text: Endless regret and keep monitoring the graph while stressed
Delete the app, close your eyes, don't want to see your portfolio anymore
Find a way to cut loss or find a side job to cover the loss

questions.question: Your room/desk is extremely messy...

options.text: Stressed seeing it but confused where to start cleaning
Leave it, sleep in the remaining empty space
Suddenly go on a cleaning rampage, clean everything quickly and throw things away

questions.question: A friend reminds you of a debt you owe but you don't have the money yet...

options.text: Anxious, extremely embarrassed, and panic thinking of borrowing elsewhere
Pretend not to have read the message (ghost) until payday
Reply honestly that you don't have it yet, ask for an extension, and promise a definite date

questions.question: Very bad weather/storm when you've planned an outdoor event...

options.text: Very sad and keep complaining about bad luck all day
Cancel completely, go back inside your blanket and sleep again
Immediately move the event indoors or force it with raincoats

questions.question: In a meeting, the boss asks for volunteers for a difficult project...

options.text: Look down, weigh the risks of failure if you take it
Pretend to be busy taking notes so you're not chosen
Immediately raise your hand to take the opportunity

questions.question: Disagreeing on principles with parents/in-laws...

options.text: Keep it inside but feel very hurt and overthink
Say 'yes yes' on the outside to end it quickly, but don't actually do it
Stand your ground, argue and defend your principles firmly

questions.question: You only slept less than 3 hours last night...

options.text: Anxious and self-suggest that today will definitely be a mess
Call in sick and skip work/college to continue sleeping
Drink 3 cups of strong black coffee and force yourself to work extra hard

questions.question: When a close relative continuously complains (toxic venting)...

options.text: Absorb their negative energy and feel stressed and burdened too
Start rarely replying to their messages and avoid meeting
Interrupt their venting and force them to follow the solution you suggest

questions.question: A physical emergency occurs (e.g., fire alarm rings)...

options.text: Panic, confused, and your mind freezes (freeze)
Run to save yourself as fast as possible to the safe zone without thinking
Shout instructions to others and look for a fire extinguisher

questions.question: An expensive electronic item (laptop/phone) suddenly goes blank while being used...

options.text: Stressed thinking about unsaved data and repair costs
Close the laptop/phone, leave it for a while hoping it fixes itself tomorrow
Force press the power button repeatedly, slap it hard, or take it apart yourself

questions.question: When reading negative news/crisis on social media...

options.text: Doomscroll continuously, read comments, and also worry about the future
Close the app, block keywords, don't want to know world affairs
Join heated debates in the comments section to defend what's right

questions.question: When faced with a difficult choice (A or B)...

options.text: Make a long list of pros and cons but still can't decide
Leave the decision to someone else so you don't have to bear the burden
Trust your gut, choose one, and get ready to face the risks

questions.question: If you fail an important job interview...

options.text: Keep replaying the stupid answers you gave during the interview
Stop applying for jobs for a while because you've lost motivation
Get angry at the recruitment system and immediately apply to 10 other companies

questions.question: In the end, what does 'Stress' mean to you?

options.text: Mental torture that makes my life feel heavy and scary
A disturbance of comfort that I should avoid as much as possible
Fuel (adrenaline) that forces me to move forward
